Task: Identify solver family and parse smoke-input profile.
            Source file: examples\su2_inviscid_bump\inv_channel_smoke.cfg

            ```text
            %%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
%                                                                              %
% SU2 configuration file                                                       %
% Case description: Inviscid, internal flow over a bump in a channel           %
% Author: Thomas D. Economon                                                   %
% Institution: Stanford University                                             %
% Date: 2012.09.29                                                             %
% File Version 4.0.2 "Cardinal"                                                %
%                                                                              %
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% ------------- DIRECT, ADJOINT, AND LINEARIZED PROBLEM DEFINITION ------------%
%
% Physical governing equations (EULER, NAVIER_STOKES, NS_PLASMA)
%
SOLVER= EULER
%
% Mathematical problem (DIRECT, CONTINUOUS_ADJOINT)
MATH_PROBLEM= DIRECT
%
% Restart solution (NO, YES)
RESTART_SOL= NO

% -----

...[truncated]...

djoint
RESTART_ADJ_FILENAME= restart_adj
%
% Output file flow (w/o extension) variables
VOLUME_FILENAME= flow
%
% Output file adjoint (w/o extension) variables
VOLUME_ADJ_FILENAME= adjoint
%
% Output objective function gradient (using continuous adjoint)
GRAD_OBJFUNC_FILENAME= of_grad.dat
%
% Output file surface flow coefficient (w/o extension)
SURFACE_FILENAME= surface_flow
%
% Output file surface adjoint coefficient (w/o extension)
SURFACE_ADJ_FILENAME= surface_adjoint
%
%
% Screen output
SCREEN_OUTPUT= (INNER_ITER, WALL_TIME, RMS_DENSITY, RMS_ENERGY, LIFT, DRAG)
            ```

            Return strict JSON with keys:
            solver, input_kind, likely_primary_log.
{"input_kind": "cfg", "likely_primary_log": "docker-su2.log", "solver": "su2"}

Task: Identify solver family and parse smoke-input profile.
            Source file: examples\su2_elasticity_smoke\case.cfg

            ```text
            % Physics
SOLVER= ELASTICITY
TIME_DOMAIN= NO
INNER_ITER= 1
%
% Optimization
TOPOLOGY_OPTIMIZATION= YES
FEA_FILENAME= element_properties.dat
TOPOL_OPTIM_FILTER_RADIUS= 0.013
TOPOL_OPTIM_FILTER_KERNEL= ( DILATE,ERODE )
TOPOL_OPTIM_KERNEL_PARAM= 0.01
TOPOL_OPTIM_PROJECTION_TYPE= NO_PROJECTION
TOPOL_OPTIM_PROJECTION_PARAM= 0.0
TOPOL_OPTIM_SIMP_EXPONENT= 3.0
TOPOL_OPTIM_OUTFILE= grad.dat
%
OBJECTIVE_FUNCTION= REFERENCE_NODE
REFERENCE_NODE= 890
REFERENCE_NODE_DISPLACEMENT= (0.0, 0.0)
REFERENCE_NODE_PENALTY= 1.0
DESIGN_VARIABLE_FEA= YOUNG_MODULUS
%
% Numerics
GEOMETRIC_CONDITIONS= SMALL_DEFORMATIONS
FORMULATION_ELASTICITY_2D= PLANE_STRESS
%
% Material properties
MATERIAL_MODEL= LINEAR_ELASTIC
ELASTICITY_MODULUS= 1070
POISSON_RATIO= 0.3
MATERIAL_DENSITY= 1000
%
% Boundary conditions
MARKER_CLAMPED= ( clamped )
MARKER_PRESSURE= ( free,0.0 )
MARKER_LOAD= ( load, 1, 1, 0, -1, 0)
%
% Linear solver
LINEAR_SOLVER= CONJUGATE_GRADIENT
LINEAR_SOLVER_PREC= ILU
LINEAR_SOLVER_ERROR= 1E-9
LINEAR_SOLVER_ITER= 1000
DISCADJ_LIN_SOLVER= CONJUGATE_GRADIENT
DISCADJ_LIN_PREC= ILU
%
% In/Out
MESH_FILENAME= mesh.su2
MESH_FORMAT= SU2
SOLUTION_FILENAME= solution.dat
RESTART_FILENAME= solution.dat
OUTPUT_FILES= RESTART
HISTORY_OUTPUT= STRUCT_COEFF
            ```

            Return strict JSON with keys:
            solver, input_kind, likely_primary_log.
{"input_kind": "cfg", "likely_primary_log": "docker-su2.log", "solver": "su2"}
